priority_rationale: Required before immigrant community channel can function. No Spanish = no community distribution.
notes: First document to translate. Review by immigration attorney or community navigator with Spanish fluency required.

priority_rationale: Immigrant-DV population overlap is high. NNEDV member orgs serve significant Spanish-speaking populations.
notes: Safety warning at top must be reviewed carefully — hotline numbers and safety phrasing must match Spanish-language DV advocacy conventions.

priority_rationale: UFW and NDWA channels require Spanish. Agricultural and domestic worker organizing is heavily Spanish-speaking.
notes: Term 'union organizing' renders as 'sindicalismo' or 'organización sindical' — translator should choose term familiar to target sector.

priority_rationale: Broadest distribution; Spanish version needed for Reddit and community sharing.
notes: Lowest complexity of all guides. Good starting document for a new translator to calibrate style.

priority_rationale: Some jurisdictions with large Spanish-speaking populations need Spanish materials for poll worker training.
notes: Election-specific vocabulary (election management system, provisional ballot) requires translator familiarity with election administration terms.

priority_rationale: Spanish-language journalism outlets (Univision, Telemundo affiliates, independent press) are a secondary channel.
notes: Technical terms (SecureDrop, Signal, end-to-end encryption) should be translated with English terms in parentheses on first use.

document_title: Implementation Guide — Part 0 (Data Broker Opt-Outs only)
priority_rationale: Part 0 is the single highest-impact section for Tier 3 audiences. A standalone Spanish version is more useful than the full guide.
notes: URLs in the opt-out list should be retained in English (the websites themselves are in English). Add a note that the process requires reading some English content.

priority_rationale: DV advocates serving Spanish-speaking communities need the full playbook for staff training — not just the quick-start.
notes: Safety warning at top is critical — must be reviewed by DV advocacy professional with Spanish fluency before distribution. Not a machine-translation-only document.

priority_rationale: For immigration attorneys and legal aid workshop facilitators conducting sessions in Spanish. Full playbook enables Spanish-language workshops.
notes: Section 1 (threat model) contains many proper nouns (ELITE, ImmigrationOS, Penlink) — translator notes should explain these are system names and should not be translated.

priority_rationale: For labor organizers and activist groups conducting security trainings in Spanish.
notes: Protest-specific vocabulary (geofencing, ALPR, facial recognition) should include parenthetical English terms for technical accuracy.

priority_rationale: Used primarily by organizations with English-capable staff. Lower direct-to-individual utility than playbooks above.
notes: Full technical document — translator should have technology background or work with a technical reviewer. Do not prioritize until Ranks 1–10 are complete.

priority_rationale: Step-by-step technical guide. Most useful when a facilitator is walking through it with a client. Full translation is a long-horizon project.
notes: Command-line instructions and app-specific menu paths should remain in English with Spanish annotations explaining what to look for.

priority_rationale: Spanish-language journalism is a real sector but smaller than immigrant/DV/labor channels. Lower priority than community-facing docs.
notes: CPJ and Freedom of the Press Foundation both have existing Spanish-language resources that can be cross-referenced.

priority_rationale: Whistleblowing guides are primarily used by English-speaking federal employees and contractors. Lower Spanish-language urgency.
notes: Legal vocabulary (qui tam, False Claims Act, Office of Special Counsel) is complex — translator must be familiar with U.S. legal system terminology.

priority_rationale: Financial privacy concepts are complex. Spanish translation useful for nonprofit and mutual aid organizations serving Spanish-speaking communities, but lower urgency than safety-focused docs.
notes: IRS and FinCEN terminology should be translated with English acronyms retained (IRS LCA, FinCEN SAR) on first use, then Spanish term alone thereafter.

priority_rationale: Priority 1–6 (quick-starts): ~18 hours total. Priority 7 (Part 0 excerpt): 8 hours. Priority 8–15 (full playbooks): ~249 hours total.
notes: Recommend phasing: Sprint 1 (weeks 1–2) = Priority 1–7 (~26 hours). Sprint 2 (months 2–3) = Priority 8–10 (~77 hours). Sprint 3 (months 4–6) = Priority 11–15 (~172 hours).
